factors.summary: Productivity is improving due to lower materials costs and higher net sales, but is being partially offset by increased labor and overhead costs.

evidence.text: Gross profit increased compared with the prior period due primarily to the contributions of the QSC acquisition, fall through of higher net sales, and lower materials cost. This increase was partially offset by increased labor and overhead costs.
evidence.subsection: First Six Months of Fiscal 2025 Compared with First Six Months of Fiscal 2024

factors.summary: Interest rate changes are negatively impacting the company, as net interest expense has increased due to higher interest-bearing debt related to the QSC acquisition.

evidence.text: We reported net interest expense of $2.9 million and $0.8 million for the six months ended February 28, 2025 and February 29, 2024, respectively. The increase in net interest expense was due to interest incurred on our $600.0 million Term Loan Facility and lower interest-bearing cash and cash equivalent balances as a result of our purchase of QSC.
evidence.subsection: First Six Months of Fiscal 2025 Compared with First Six Months of Fiscal 2024

factors.summary: The company is facing challenges in pricing power as higher costs may not be fully offset by price increases.

evidence.text: Although we have taken actions to mitigate the impacts of tariffs and trade restrictions, including pricing actions, competitive pricing or market pressures may not allow us to pass on the additional cost of these tariffs and may adversely affect our profit margins, results of operations, and/or cash flows.
evidence.subsection: Financial Condition, Capital Resources, and Liquidity

factors.summary: CapEx is stable with investments primarily in new and enhanced information technology.

evidence.text: We invested $28.6 million and $29.0 million in property, plant, and equipment during the six months ended February 28, 2025 and February 29, 2024, respectively. We invested primarily in new and enhanced information technology, equipment, tooling, and facility improvements in fiscal 2025 to date.
evidence.subsection: Capital Allocation Priorities

factors.summary: Debt leverage is stable with no significant changes in outstanding debt balance.

evidence.text: Our outstanding debt balance was $1.1 billion, which consisted of our Unsecured Notes and borrowings on our Term Loan Facility, compared to our cash position of $397.9 million. We were in compliance with all covenants under our financing arrangements as of February 28, 2025.
evidence.subsection: Financial Condition, Capital Resources, and Liquidity

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are maintained with consistent dividend payments and share repurchases.

evidence.text: We paid dividends on our common stock of $10.0 million ($0.32 per share) and $8.8 million ($0.28 per share) during the six months ended February 28, 2025 and February 29, 2024, respectively. [...] We expect to repurchase shares on an opportunistic basis subject to various factors including stock price, Company performance, market conditions, and other possible uses of cash.
evidence.subsection: Capital Allocation Priorities

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is decreasing due to higher payments for inventory and acquisition-related costs.

evidence.text: Cash generated from operating activities and cash on hand were used during the current year to partially fund the QSC, LLC (“QSC”) acquisition and our other capital allocation priorities as discussed below. [...] This decrease reflects higher payments for purchases of inventory, the timing of sales and cash collections from our customers, payments for acquisition-related costs, and higher tax payments.
evidence.subsection: Financial Condition, Capital Resources, and Liquidity

factors.summary: Acquisitions are creating value through expansion into new markets and product lines.

evidence.text: We acquired all of the equity interests of QSC, a leader in the design, engineering, and manufacturing of audio, video, and control solutions and services, for $1.2 billion. [...] This acquisition is intended to expand AIS into a cloud-manageable audio, video, and control platform that includes controls, sensors, and software with broad applications across multiple end-markets including education, commercial, hospitality, government, healthcare, and transportation.
evidence.subsection: Capital Allocation Priorities

factors.summary: Management is confident with their capital allocation priorities and liquidity position.

evidence.text: We believe that we will be able to meet our liquidity needs over the next 12 months based on our cash on hand, current projections of cash flows from operations, borrowing availability under financing arrangements, and current access to capital markets. [...] Our cash flows from operations and sources of funding, including, but not limited to, future borrowings and borrowing capacity, will sufficiently support our long-term liquidity needs.
evidence.subsection: Financial Condition, Capital Resources, and Liquidity

factors.summary: Near-term risks include potential adverse impacts from tariffs and trade restrictions.

evidence.text: The U.S. government has imposed, and is considering imposing, additional tariffs and trade restrictions on certain goods produced outside of the United States, including steel and aluminum. [...] The situation concerning the imposition of additional tariffs and trade restrictions by the U.S. and other jurisdictions [...] could adversely impact demand for our products, costs, inflation, customers, suppliers, and the U.S. economy. Although we have taken actions to mitigate the impacts of tariffs and trade restrictions, including pricing actions, competitive pricing or market pressures may not allow us to pass on the additional cost of these tariffs and may adversely affect our profit margins, results of operations, and/or cash flows.
evidence.subsection: Capital Allocation Priorities

factors.summary: Long-term strategy is focused on growth through innovation and entering attractive new verticals.

evidence.text: We achieve growth through the development of innovative new products and services, including lighting, lighting controls, building management solutions, and an audio, video and control platform. [...] We look to aggressively deploy capital to grow the business and to enter attractive new verticals.
evidence.subsection: Company

factors.summary: The company is sensitive to changes in tariffs and trade policies, which could impact costs and demand.

evidence.text: The situation concerning the imposition of additional tariffs and trade restrictions by the U.S. and other jurisdictions [...] could adversely impact demand for our products, costs, inflation, customers, suppliers, and the U.S. economy. Although we have taken actions to mitigate the impacts of tariffs and trade restrictions, including pricing actions, competitive pricing or market pressures may not allow us to pass on the additional cost of these tariffs and may adversely affect our profit margins, results of operations, and/or cash flows.
evidence.subsection: Capital Allocation Priorities

factors.summary: The QSC acquisition is a near-term catalyst for expanding into new markets and product lines.

evidence.text: This acquisition is intended to expand AIS into a cloud-manageable audio, video, and control platform that includes controls, sensors, and software with broad applications across multiple end-markets including education, commercial, hospitality, government, healthcare, and transportation.
evidence.subsection: Capital Allocation Priorities

factors.summary: The company's organic revenue growth is decelerating, as evidenced by a decrease in the Acuity Brands Lighting segment's net sales. While overall net sales increased due to the acquisition of QSC, the organic performance shows a decline.

evidence.text: Net sales for the six months ended February 28, 2025 increased $117.3 million, or 6.4%, to $1,957.9 million compared with $1,840.6 million in the prior-year period. The increase was primarily driven by the acquisition of QSC, which contributed $95.1 million in revenue to the Acuity Intelligent Spaces segment, as well as higher net sales of our Distech and Atrius products.
evidence.subsection: First Six Months of Fiscal 2025 Compared with First Six Months of Fiscal 2024

evidence.text: Acuity Brands Lighting net sales for the second quarter of fiscal 2025 decreased $2.9 million, or 0.3%, to $840.6 million, compared with $843.5 million in the prior-year period.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter of Fiscal 2025 Compared with Second Quarter of Fiscal 2024

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly driven by the QSC acquisition and improvements in products within the Acuity Intelligent Spaces segment. This shift has resulted in increased net sales and operating profits for Acuity Intelligent Spaces, contributing positively to the overall revenue mix.

evidence.text: Acuity Intelligent Spaces net sales for the six months ended February 28, 2025 increased 85.2% compared with the prior-year period. The increase in sales is attributed primarily to the acquisition of QSC, which contributed $95.1 million in revenues, as well as improvements in Distech and Atrius products. Acuity Intelligent Spaces operating profit was $20.7 million for the six months ended February 28, 2025 compared with $14.4 million in the prior-year period, an increase of $6.3 million.
evidence.subsection: First Six Months of Fiscal 2025 Compared with First Six Months of Fiscal 2024

evidence.text: The increase in gross profit was due primarily to the contributions of the QSC acquisition as well as our strategic management of the relationship between price and cost escalations, including higher labor, overhead, and Asian-sourced finished goods costs.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter of Fiscal 2025 Compared with Second Quarter of Fiscal 2024

factors.summary: Overall demand is stable to slightly increasing, with mixed performance across different segments. While there is a positive trend in net sales and contributions from new acquisitions and product lines, certain segments experienced a decrease in sales.

evidence.text: Net sales for the six months ended February 28, 2025 increased $117.3 million, or 6.4%, to $1,957.9 million compared with $1,840.6 million in the prior-year period. The increase was primarily driven by the acquisition of QSC, which contributed $95.1 million in revenue to the Acuity Intelligent Spaces segment, as well as higher net sales of our Distech and Atrius products.
evidence.subsection: First Six Months of Fiscal 2025 Compared with First Six Months of Fiscal 2024

evidence.text: Net sales for the second quarter of fiscal 2025 increased $100.4 million, or 11.1%, to $1.0 billion, compared with $905.9 million in the prior-year period due to an increase in sales in our Acuity Intelligent Spaces segment driven by the acquisition of QSC, which contributed $95.1 million in sales, as well higher net sales in our Distech and Atrius products. These increases were partially offset by a decrease in sales in our Acuity Brands Lighting segment.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter of Fiscal 2025 Compared with Second Quarter of Fiscal 2024

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing in the Acuity Intelligent Spaces segment, driven by the acquisition of QSC and higher sales of Distech and Atrius products. This growth is consistent across both the first six months and the second quarter of fiscal 2025 compared to the respective prior periods.

evidence.text: Net sales for the six months ended February 28, 2025 increased $117.3 million, or 6.4%, to $1,957.9 million compared with $1,840.6 million in the prior-year period. The increase was primarily driven by the acquisition of QSC, which contributed $95.1 million in revenue to the Acuity Intelligent Spaces segment, as well as higher net sales of our Distech and Atrius products.
evidence.subsection: First Six Months of Fiscal 2025 Compared with First Six Months of Fiscal 2024

evidence.text: Acuity Intelligent Spaces net sales for the second quarter of fiscal 2025 increased $103.4 million, or 151.8%, to $171.5 million, compared with $68.1 million in the prior-year period. The increase in sales within the Acuity Intelligent Spaces segment is attributed primarily to the acquisition of QSC, which contributed $95.1 million in revenues.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter of Fiscal 2025 Compared with Second Quarter of Fiscal 2024

factors.summary: The Acuity Intelligent Spaces segment is driving strong growth, particularly due to the QSC acquisition and improved product sales. However, the Acuity Brands Lighting segment shows minimal growth.

evidence.text: Acuity Intelligent Spaces net sales for the six months ended February 28, 2025 increased 85.2% compared with the prior-year period. The increase in sales is attributed primarily to the acquisition of QSC, which contributed $95.1 million in revenues, as well as improvements in Distech and Atrius products.
evidence.subsection: First Six Months of Fiscal 2025 Compared with First Six Months of Fiscal 2024

evidence.text: This acquisition is intended to expand AIS into a cloud-manageable audio, video, and control platform that includes controls, sensors, and software with broad applications across multiple end-markets including education, commercial, hospitality, government, healthcare, and transportation.
evidence.subsection: Capital Allocation Priorities

factors.summary: Input costs have shown mixed trends, with declining costs contributing to higher gross profit margins in the first six months of fiscal 2025 compared to the previous year. However, in the second quarter, input costs, particularly labor, overhead, and Asian-sourced finished goods costs, have risen, which the company is managing through strategic pricing.

evidence.text: Gross profit margin increased 120 basis points to 46.9% for the six months ended February 28, 2025 compared with 45.7% in the prior-year period. Our gross profit increased compared with the prior period due primarily to the contributions of the QSC acquisition, fall through of higher net sales, and lower materials cost.
evidence.subsection: First Six Months of Fiscal 2025 Compared with First Six Months of Fiscal 2024

evidence.text: Our gross profit increased compared with the prior period due primarily to the contributions of the QSC acquisition as well as our strategic management of the relationship between price and cost escalations, including higher labor, overhead, and Asian-sourced finished goods costs.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter of Fiscal 2025 Compared with Second Quarter of Fiscal 2024

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, leading to margin pressure. Wage inflation and overhead increases are contributing to higher expenses, particularly in selling, distribution, and administrative areas. The QSC acquisition has also added to these costs.

evidence.text: Selling, distribution, and administrative expenses for the six months ended February 28, 2025 were $673.8 million compared with $589.8 million in the prior-year period, an increase of $84.0 million, or 14.2%. The increase in SD&A expenses was due primarily to the addition of QSC operating expenses, higher employee-related costs, acquisition-related costs, and higher amortization of acquired intangible assets in connection with the QSC acquisition.
evidence.subsection: First Six Months of Fiscal 2025 Compared with First Six Months of Fiscal 2024

evidence.text: Our gross profit increased compared with the prior period due primarily to the contributions of the QSC acquisition as well as our strategic management of the relationship between price and cost escalations, including higher labor, overhead, and Asian-sourced finished goods costs.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter of Fiscal 2025 Compared with Second Quarter of Fiscal 2024

factors.summary: The company is experiencing mixed margin performance. While gross margins are expanding, driven by lower materials costs and higher net sales, operating margins are under pressure due to higher selling, distribution, and administrative expenses. This results in a decrease in operating profit despite the increase in gross profit.

evidence.text: Gross profit margin increased 120 basis points to 46.9% for the six months ended February 28, 2025 compared with 45.7% in the prior-year period.
evidence.subsection: First Six Months of Fiscal 2025 Compared with First Six Months of Fiscal 2024

evidence.text: Operating profit for the first six months of fiscal 2025 was $243.5 million (12.4% of net sales) compared with $251.0 million (13.6% of net sales) for the prior-year period, a decrease of $7.5 million, or 3.0%. The decrease in operating profit was due to higher SD&A expenses, partially offset by higher gross profit.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter of Fiscal 2025 Compared with Second Quarter of Fiscal 2024

evidence.text: Gross profit margin increased 100 basis points to 46.5% from 45.5% compared with the prior-year period.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter of Fiscal 2025 Compared with Second Quarter of Fiscal 2024

evidence.text: Operating profit margin decreased 200 basis points to 11.0% from 13.0% of net sales.
evidence.subsection: Second Quarter of Fiscal 2025 Compared with Second Quarter of Fiscal 2024
